chapters.titleZh: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities
chapters.titleEn: Chapter 1 — EMS Systems, Roles, and Responsibilities

chapters.titleZh: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic
chapters.titleEn: Chapter 2 — The Well-Being of the Paramedic

questions.questionZh: 在 EMS 系统中，paramedic 角色最核心的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core significance of the paramedic role within an EMS system?

options.zh: 只负责把病人送到医院
options.en: To only transport patients to hospital

options.zh: 在系统框架内进行评估、干预、转运与专业协作
options.en: To assess, intervene, transport, and work professionally within a system framework

options.zh: 完全独立于医疗指挥之外工作
options.en: To work completely independently from medical direction

options.zh: 只处理创伤病人
options.en: To deal only with trauma patients

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 的核心不是把 paramedic 当成单纯搬运角色，而是强调其在 EMS system 中的专业定位、责任、医疗指挥关系与质量框架。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 1 frames the paramedic as a professional within a system—responsible for assessment, intervention, transport, accountability, and integration with medical direction and quality improvement.

questions.questionZh: 下列哪一项最能说明 airway、oxygenation、ventilation 三者的关系？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best reflects the relationship among airway, oxygenation, and ventilation?

options.zh: 它们完全是同一个概念
options.en: They are exactly the same concept

options.zh: 只要有氧气瓶就代表 ventilation 足够
options.en: Having an oxygen tank means ventilation is adequate

options.zh: 它们彼此相关，但不是完全相同的问题
options.en: They are closely related, but not identical problems

options.zh: 只要病人能说话，就说明三者都没问题
options.en: If the patient can speak, all three are definitely fine

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 的核心之一，就是分清 airway 通不通、氧气有没有送进去、通气够不够，这三者相关但并不完全相同。
questions.explanationEn: A central learning point in Chapter 13 is that airway patency, oxygenation, and ventilation are related but distinct clinical concepts.

questions.questionZh: Patient assessment 最核心的意义是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the central purpose of patient assessment?

options.zh: 把 history、exam、scene 与 ongoing reassessment 串成完整判断流程
options.en: To connect history, exam, scene information, and reassessment into a complete decision process

options.zh: 只记录生命体征
options.en: To record vital signs only

options.zh: 跳过病史直接做所有操作
options.en: To skip history and perform all interventions immediately

options.zh: 只在 hospital 才有意义
options.en: It is only meaningful in hospital settings

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 是整本书的方法论主轴，强调 assessment 是把 scene、病史、查体、持续评估整合起来，而不是做单一动作。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 17 acts as a methodological spine for the book, integrating scene findings, history, physical exam, and ongoing reassessment into a coherent clinical process.

questions.questionZh: Chapter 2 强调 paramedic well-being 的主要原因是什么？
questions.questionEn: Why does Chapter 2 place strong emphasis on paramedic well-being?

options.zh: 因为临床技能不重要
options.en: Because clinical skills are unimportant

options.zh: 因为急救人员的身心状态会直接影响现场安全、判断与长期职业稳定性
options.en: Because provider well-being directly affects scene safety, judgment, and long-term professional sustainability

options.zh: 因为只有管理层需要关心压力
options.en: Because only managers need to care about stress

options.zh: 因为它主要是行政要求
options.en: Because it is mainly an administrative requirement

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 不是软性附录，而是在强调压力、死亡暴露、自我保护与职业耐久性都会直接影响 paramedic 的判断和安全。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 is not a soft add-on; it emphasizes that stress, exposure to death, self-protection, and resilience directly affect judgment and safety in paramedic practice.

questions.questionZh: Illness and Injury Prevention 这一章最想扩展 EMS 的哪一种角色？
questions.questionEn: What EMS role does the Illness and Injury Prevention chapter primarily expand?

options.zh: 只做反应式抢救
options.en: Purely reactive emergency response

options.zh: 把 EMS 延伸到社区预防与公共健康参与
options.en: Extending EMS into community prevention and public health involvement

options.zh: 让 paramedic 代替所有家庭医生工作
options.en: Having paramedics replace all family physician functions

options.zh: 只负责交通运输
options.en: Transport only

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 3 的重点是：paramedic 不只是事后救援，也能参与减少伤病发生的前端预防工作。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 3 emphasizes that paramedics are not only responders after harm occurs; they can also contribute to prevention and public health efforts upstream.

questions.questionZh: 在 Medical and Legal Issues 中，documentation 为什么特别重要？
questions.questionEn: Why is documentation especially important in Medical and Legal Issues?

options.zh: 只是为了让报告看起来完整
options.en: Only to make reports look complete

options.zh: 因为它同时关系到连续照护、法律责任与专业可追溯性
options.en: Because it supports continuity of care, legal accountability, and professional traceability

options.zh: 只在 hospital 内部有意义
options.en: It only matters inside hospitals

options.zh: 只要做了就不用记
options.en: If care was provided, recording it is unnecessary

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 明确把 documentation 放在法律与专业责任框架内看，不只是记录，更是护理连续性和法律保护的一部分。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 frames documentation as part of legal accountability and professional duty, not merely note-taking.

questions.questionZh: Ethical Issues 这一章最核心的训练是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core skill developed in the Ethical Issues chapter?

options.zh: 只背固定答案
options.en: Memorizing fixed answers only

options.zh: 在灰区中用伦理框架进行专业判断
options.en: Using ethical frameworks to make professional judgments in gray-zone situations

options.zh: 避免与病人沟通
options.en: Avoiding communication with patients

options.zh: 只关心器官捐赠流程
options.en: Only focusing on organ donation procedures

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 5 的重点不是提供所有情境的单一标准答案，而是训练 autonomy、resuscitation decisions、professional integrity 等伦理判断。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 5 is less about one correct answer for every scenario and more about building ethical reasoning around autonomy, resuscitation decisions, and professional integrity.

questions.questionZh: Medical direction 在 EMS 系统中的意义，最准确的描述是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best describes the role of medical direction in an EMS system?

options.zh: 它表示 paramedic 完全不能独立思考
options.en: It means paramedics cannot think independently

options.zh: 它把现场照护与更大的医疗系统、标准和监督连接起来
options.en: It connects field care with the broader medical system, standards, and oversight

options.zh: 它只在 paperwork 上有作用
options.en: It only matters for paperwork

options.zh: 它等同于 police command
options.en: It is the same as police command

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 1 中 medical direction 的核心，是让院前照护不脱离医疗系统，而是在协议、质量和监督框架下运作。
questions.explanationEn: In Chapter 1, medical direction ensures prehospital care remains integrated with medical standards, protocols, oversight, and quality structures.

questions.questionZh: 下列哪一项最符合 negligence（过失）相关风险的理解？
questions.questionEn: Which option best reflects the idea of negligence risk?

options.zh: 只要主观上是好意，就不可能构成问题
options.en: Good intentions automatically eliminate negligence risk

options.zh: 只要病人最后没事，就没有法律问题
options.en: If the patient ends up okay, there is no legal issue

options.zh: 专业责任、行为标准、记录与结果之间都可能构成过失争议
options.en: Professional duty, standard of care, documentation, and outcomes may all become part of negligence claims

options.zh: 只有医生才会涉及 negligence
options.en: Only physicians face negligence issues

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 4 强调 paramedic 同样有 legal accountability，过失并不是只看动机，而是看 duty、standard、breach、damage 等整体框架。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 4 emphasizes that paramedics also carry legal accountability; negligence is assessed through duty, standards, breach, documentation, and resulting harm—not intent alone.

questions.questionZh: 面对高压、死亡暴露和持续轮班，Chapter 2 最核心的态度是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is the core attitude promoted in Chapter 2 when facing stress, death exposure, and shift work?

options.zh: 硬撑就好，不需要处理
options.en: Just push through; no active management is needed

options.zh: 这些都只是个人软弱问题
options.en: These are merely signs of personal weakness

options.zh: paramedic 需要主动识别、管理并建立保护机制
options.en: Paramedics should actively recognize, manage, and build protective strategies

options.zh: 只在下班后才和工作有关
options.en: These only matter after the shift ends

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 2 把 well-being 当成专业能力的一部分，而不是可有可无的附属话题。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 2 treats well-being as part of professional competence, not as an optional side issue.

questions.questionZh: 在 airway assessment 中，最危险的错误之一是什么？
questions.questionEn: What is one of the most dangerous errors during airway assessment?

options.zh: 把能说几句话误当成绝对安全
options.en: Assuming a patient is fully safe simply because they can speak a few words

options.zh: 先看病人整体外观
options.en: Looking at the overall presentation first

options.zh: 注意 suction 是否需要
options.en: Recognizing that suction may be needed

options.zh: 区分 airway 与 ventilation 问题
options.en: Distinguishing airway problems from ventilation problems

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 13 强调 airway 状态可能快速恶化，能说话不代表后续不会崩。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 13 emphasizes that airway status may deteriorate rapidly; the ability to speak does not guarantee ongoing airway safety.

questions.questionZh: Patient assessment 的价值最准确的说法是哪一个？
questions.questionEn: Which statement best captures the value of patient assessment?

options.zh: 只做一次 initial exam 就够了
options.en: A single initial exam is enough

options.zh: 它是把 scene、history、exam 和 reassessment 串成连续临床判断
options.en: It links scene findings, history, exam, and reassessment into a continuous clinical judgment process

options.zh: 它主要是为了写 paperwork
options.en: It exists mainly for paperwork

options.zh: 它只适用于 trauma
options.en: It only applies to trauma

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 17 的核心是连续评估，不是只做一个静态检查。
questions.explanationEn: The core of Chapter 17 is dynamic, ongoing assessment—not a one-time static check.

questions.questionZh: Clinical decisions 这一章最强调哪种能力？
questions.questionEn: What ability is most emphasized in the Clinical Decisions chapter?

options.zh: 背出所有标准答案
options.en: Memorizing every standard answer

options.zh: 把知识转化成优先级判断与实际行动
options.en: Turning knowledge into prioritization and practical action

options.zh: 只按感觉做决定
options.en: Making decisions based purely on intuition

options.zh: 完全跳过 reassessment
options.en: Completely skipping reassessment

questions.explanationZh: Chapter 18 的重点是 critical thinking 和 real-world decision making。
questions.explanationEn: Chapter 18 focuses on critical thinking and real-world decision making, not rote memorization alone.
